text: This is the VOA Special English Health Report.
zh: 这里是美国之音特别英语健康报道。

text: The International Red Cross movement grew out of a major battle in the unification of Italy.
zh: 国际红十字运动源于意大利统一战争中的一场重要战役。

text: The Battle of Solferino took place 150 years ago in June of 1859.
zh: 索尔费里诺战役发生在150年前,即1859年6月。

text: Volunteers from Red Cross and Red Crescent societies around the world gathered in Solferino to mark the anniversary.
zh: 来自世界各地红十字会与红新月会的志愿者齐聚索尔费里诺,纪念这一周年。

text: About 8 ,000 people marched in a torch -lit event called a fiaccolata.
zh: 约8000人参加了一场名为“fiaccolata”的火炬游行活动。

text: They followed in the footsteps of those who took injured soldiers from Solferino to the nearest village, Castiglione.
zh: 他们沿着当年将伤兵从索尔费里诺转移到最近的村庄卡斯蒂廖内的路线前行。

text: Hanegton Sigrinya, National Youth President
zh: 乌干达红十字会全国青年主席哈尼格顿·西格里尼亚

text: of the Ugandan Red Cross Society, says the visit made him want to work harder to help people.
zh: 表示,这次探访让他更想努力去帮助他人。

text: He said he is going back to Uganda to work for humanity.
zh: 他说他将回到乌干达,为人道事业工作。

text: He said when young people come together, they have the possibility of doing whatever it takes to make the world a better place.
zh: 他说,当年轻人团结在一起时,他们就有可能不惜一切让世界变得更美好。

text: In the battle, Allied French and Sardinian troops defeated the Austrian army.
zh: 在这场战役中,法国与撒丁联军击败了奥地利军队。

text: Around 6 ,000 men were killed and more than 30 ,000 were wounded.
zh: 约有6000人阵亡,超过3万人受伤。

text: Yet, says Swiss historian François Bougnon,
zh: 然而,瑞士历史学家弗朗索瓦·布农指出,

text: the battle lasted only twelve hours he says a Swiss businessman named Henri Dunant was horrified by what he saw
zh: 这场战役仅持续了十二个小时。他说,一位名叫亨利·杜南的瑞士商人被眼前的景象深深震惊。

text: thousands of wounded were brought to the next town of Castiglione there was almost no medical assistance mr.
zh: 成千上万的伤员被送到附近的卡斯蒂廖内镇,而那里几乎没有任何医疗救助。

text: Dunant saw thousands of men suffering from very deep wounds and left to die without any real assistance.
zh: 杜南目睹成千上万的士兵伤势深重,却无人救助,只能等死。

text: Mr. Bunyan says Henri Dunant quickly took action.
zh: 布农说,亨利·杜南迅速采取了行动。

text: The businessman got local women to provide food and water.
zh: 这位商人组织当地妇女提供食物和水。

text: He also got them to dress the wounds of soldiers without concern for their nationality.
zh: 他还让她们不分国籍地为伤兵包扎伤口。

text: Mr. Dunant later wrote a book called A Memory of Solferino.
zh: 杜南后来写了一本书,名为《索尔费里诺回忆录》。

text: In it, he launched two ideas.
zh: 在书中,他提出了两个构想。

text: One was the idea of voluntary relief societies to provide assistance to the wounded or other people.
zh: 其一是设立志愿救援团体,为伤员及其他有需要的人提供帮助。

text: This led to the Red Cross and Red Crescent movement.
zh: 这一构想催生了红十字与红新月运动。

text: The second idea was a treaty protecting the wounded and medical personnel on the field of battle.
zh: 第二个构想是订立条约,保护战场上的伤员和医护人员。

text: This, explains historian Francois Bouillon, is the origin of the Geneva Conventions.
zh: 历史学家弗朗索瓦·布农解释说,这正是《日内瓦公约》的起源。

text: Stephen Ryan is the communications officer for for youth and volunteers at the International Federation of Red Cross and Red Crescent Societies.
zh: 斯蒂芬·瑞安是国际红十字与红新月会联合会青年与志愿者事务的新闻官员。

text: He says it is important to get young people involved in volunteer work at an early age.
zh: 他说,让年轻人从小就参与志愿工作十分重要。

text: He says young people need to be given the chance to feel like they are making a difference in the world.
zh: 他说,应该让年轻人有机会切身感受到自己正在为世界带来改变。

text: And that's the VOA Special English Health Report.
zh: 以上就是美国之音特别英语健康报道。
